description:
You are assisting a researcher who is tidying up a collection-download log.  
    Everything you need is already under /home/user/datasets/.

    Current data layout (already present before you start):
      • /home/user/datasets/download.log     ← the raw log you must analyse
      • /home/user/datasets/                 ← writable directory for all new files

    Log format (one entry per line, already in download.log):
        [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] STATUS DATASET_ID SIZE
      - STATUS is either OK or FAIL.
      - SIZE is written with “MB” at the end for OK lines (e.g. 152.3MB).
        For FAIL lines it is always a single dash “-”.

    Your tasks (≈ 5–10 shell commands in total):
      1. Read /home/user/datasets/download.log and extract the statistics below.
      2. Create a CSV summary called /home/user/datasets/summary_report.csv  
         using exactly this three-row, two-column layout (no extra spaces, tabs,
         quotes, or blank lines):
            metric,value
            success_count,<NUMBER_OF_OK_LINES>
            failure_count,<NUMBER_OF_FAIL_LINES>
            total_size_mb,<SUM_OF_SIZES_FROM_OK_LINES_WITH_TWO_DECIMALS>
         • total_size_mb must be the arithmetic sum of the numeric SIZE fields
           from all OK entries, expressed in megabytes with exactly two digits
           after the decimal point (round half-up when necessary).

      3. Create an error extract file called /home/user/datasets/errors.log that
         contains only the original FAIL lines, in the same chronological order
         as they appear in download.log, unmodified and one per line.

    Acceptance criteria checked by the automatic grader:
      • /home/user/datasets/summary_report.csv exists and is readable.
      • The file content is exactly four lines, comma-separated, in the order
        shown above, and the numeric values are correct.
      • /home/user/datasets/errors.log exists and holds every FAIL line from
        download.log, in the same order, with no extra or missing lines.
      • No other files are created or modified in /home/user/datasets/.
truth:
Initial state that already exists before the agent starts:
      Directory: /home/user/datasets/

      File: /home/user/datasets/download.log
      Contents (15 lines, Unix line endings):
        [2023-06-01 10:15:23] OK imagenet_1 152.3MB
        [2023-06-01 10:16:05] OK imagenet_2 153.1MB
        [2023-06-01 10:16:45] FAIL coco_2017 -
        [2023-06-01 10:17:12] OK voc_2012 2.1MB
        [2023-06-01 10:18:01] FAIL places365_basic -
        [2023-06-01 10:18:34] OK cifar10 0.16MB
        [2023-06-01 10:19:10] OK cifar100 0.19MB
        [2023-06-01 10:20:22] OK mnist 0.05MB
        [2023-06-01 10:21:55] OK fashion_mnist 0.06MB
        [2023-06-01 10:22:26] FAIL kitti -
        [2023-06-02 09:12:05] OK lsun 34.2MB
        [2023-06-02 09:13:11] OK cityscapes 11.3MB
        [2023-06-02 09:14:49] OK sun397 23.5MB
        [2023-06-02 09:15:30] OK camvid 2.4MB
        [2023-06-02 09:16:10] FAIL imagenet21k -

    Expected state after a correct solution:

      File: /home/user/datasets/summary_report.csv  
      Exact content (4 lines, Unix line endings):
        metric,value
        success_count,11
        failure_count,4
        total_size_mb,379.36

      File: /home/user/datasets/errors.log  
      Exact content (4 lines, Unix line endings):
        [2023-06-01 10:16:45] FAIL coco_2017 -
        [2023-06-01 10:18:01] FAIL places365_basic -
        [2023-06-01 10:22:26] FAIL kitti -
        [2023-06-02 09:16:10] FAIL imagenet21k -

    No other files may be added, removed, or changed inside /home/user/datasets/.
